model_narrative: We are planning an ammonia-and-fertilizer supply network. We decide which domestic ammonia plants to build and which domestic fertilizer plants to build, and we decide how much product to route along each end-to-end path that starts at an ammonia source, passes through a domestic fertilizer plant, and ends at a demand center. Building a plant incurs a fixed erection cost, and every unit routed incurs a combined production-and-shipping cost along its path. The objective is to make the total cost, covering plant erection plus all routing, as small as possible.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: all sources of ammonia; member 0 is the external import source and members 1 through 5 are domestic plants

sets.name: id
sets.doc: the domestic plants only, a subset of the ammonia sources; these are the sites that can be erected to make ammonia

sets.name: k
sets.doc: the demand centers that require fertilizer

sets.name: product
sets.doc: the two products whose plants can be built, namely ammonia and fertilizer

sets.name: jd
sets.doc: the domestic fertilizer plants, an alias of the domestic plant set used as the fertilizer-production stage

params.name: fc
params.doc: the fixed cost of erecting a plant, indexed by site and by product type; only the domestic plant sites carry an erection cost

params.name: c
params.doc: the combined production and shipping cost for moving ammonia from an ammonia source to a domestic fertilizer plant, per unit

params.name: d
params.doc: the production and shipping cost for moving fertilizer from a fertilizer plant to a demand center, per unit

params.name: r
params.doc: the fertilizer demand required at each demand center, in units

vars.name: u
vars.doc: the tagged shipment quantity routed from an ammonia source through a domestic fertilizer plant to a demand center; this single flow carries the product end to end so its cost combines the ammonia leg and the fertilizer leg

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the build decision for each domestic fertilizer plant, equal to one if that fertilizer plant is erected and zero otherwise

vars.name: w
vars.doc: the build decision for each domestic ammonia plant, equal to one if that ammonia plant is erected and zero otherwise

vars.name: tc
vars.doc: the total cost over the whole network, combining plant erection costs and the production-and-shipping cost of all tagged flows

constraints.name: u[i,
constraints.intent: Every demand center must receive at least the fertilizer it needs. For each demand center, add up all the tagged flow arriving there from every ammonia source through every domestic fertilizer plant, and require that total to be at least the demand at that center.

constraints.name: r[k]
constraints.intent: An ammonia plant can only send out product if it has been built, and what it sends is limited by how much is needed. For each domestic ammonia plant and each demand center, the total tagged flow originating at that ammonia plant and bound for that demand center must not exceed the demand at that center when the plant is built, and must be zero when it is not built.

constraints.name: r[k]
constraints.intent: A fertilizer plant can only handle product if it has been built, and what passes through it is limited by how much is needed. For each domestic fertilizer plant and each demand center, the total tagged flow passing through that fertilizer plant on its way to that demand center must not exceed the demand at that center when the plant is built, and must be zero when it is not built.

constraints.name: fc[id,
constraints.intent: The total cost gathers the fixed cost of building plants together with the cost of all the routing. Charge the erection cost of each domestic ammonia plant that is built and of each domestic fertilizer plant that is built, and charge every unit of tagged flow the combined ammonia-leg and fertilizer-leg cost along its path. Set the total cost equal to the sum of all these amounts.

query: Plant 2's line to center 4 is being throttled — it can deliver at most 100 units of tagged product into that market. Cap the total flow reaching center 4 through plant 2 at 100 and tell me how the plan and cost shift.
expected_pyomo: model.cap_c4 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.u[i, 2, 4] for i in model.i) <= 100)

query: Suppose plant 2 can route no more than 200 units to center 5 because of a handling limit at that hub. Cap the tagged flow through plant 2 into center 5 at 200 and let me see the resulting cost.
expected_pyomo: model.cap_c5 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.u[i, 2, 5] for i in model.i) <= 200)

query: For a national program, concentrating output in one or two plants feels fragile. Shouldn't fertilizer production run at no fewer than three sites? What's the optimizer seeing that keeps it from spreading further?
expected_pyomo: model.min_three_fert = Constraint(expr=sum(model.z[j] for j in model.jd) >= 3)

query: Center 7's entire requirement rides on the tagged route through plant 2. Wouldn't it be more robust to cap plant 2's delivery to center 7 at 80 and pull a second source into that market? What's the tradeoff keeping it all on one route?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_c7 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.u[i, 2, 7] for i in model.i) <= 80)
